title: Pyracantha rogersiana
intro: Pyracantha rogersiana, also known as firethorn, is a thorny evergreen shrub native to China, characterized by its white flowers and red berries. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which provide potential health benefits. Bioactive constituents of Pyracantha rogersiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include tea, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and support immune function.
taxonomy: Pyracantha rogersiana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It belongs to the genus Pyracantha and is often referred to as a firethorn due to its thorny branches. This evergreen shrub is native to China and is known for its white flowers and red berries. The classification of Pyracantha rogersiana reflects its close relationship to other plants in the rose family. Its classification helps to understand its evolutionary history and its place within the plant kingdom.
